context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements the image_asset theme-asset fallback pattern in the image-banner section when Shopify Files library access is unavailable. The pattern requires three specific changes: adding a text setting to the schema, adding a Liquid branch that renders the asset_url, and using the image_asset key in template JSON instead of shopify://shop_images/ URLs.

checklist.name: image_asset text setting in schema
checklist.description: The updated image-banner.liquid adds a text setting with id "image_asset" to the schema settings array

checklist.name: Setting label present
checklist.description: The image_asset setting has a label field (e.g. 'Theme asset filename (fallback)')

checklist.name: Setting info field present
checklist.description: The image_asset setting has an info field explaining its use (e.g. 'Used when no image is picked')

checklist.name: Liquid branch for blank picker
checklist.description: The Liquid code adds a branch that checks for image_asset when the picker image is blank (e.g. elsif section.settings.image_asset != blank or equivalent)

checklist.name: asset_url filter used
checklist.description: The Liquid branch renders the image using the asset_url filter (e.g. section.settings.image_asset | asset_url)

checklist.name: CSS object-fit cover
checklist.description: The fallback img tag includes style properties for full coverage (width:100%; height:100%; object-fit:cover or equivalent)

checklist.name: Template uses image_asset key
checklist.description: templates/index.json uses the "image_asset" key with a filename value (e.g. "image_asset": "hero.png") for the image-banner section

checklist.name: No shopify://shop_images/ in template
checklist.description: templates/index.json does NOT use a "image": "shopify://shop_images/..." value for the banner (relies on image_asset instead)

checklist.name: Original image picker preserved
checklist.description: The image_picker setting and its normal rendering path are still present in the updated section (not removed)
